title: 9 Interac e-Transfer脗庐 Response Codes
body_text:
9	Interac e-Transfer
脗庐
Response Codes
9.1	Overview of Interac
e-Transfer Response Codes
Central 1芒聙聶s system is set up to recognize a variety of response codes from the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system and from Interac.
When an Interac e-Transfer customer attempts to send or fulfill an Interac e-Transfer transaction via online banking,
the online banking platform sends the request to the Interac e-Transfer application within Central 1芒聙聶s system;
the Interac e-Transfer application sends a debit proposal and debit request to the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system;
if the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system sends a response code that Central 1 recognizes as successful, Central 1 sends a request to Interac to create the Interac e-Transfer transaction;
Interac sends a response code to Central 1 that determines if the Interac e-Transfer transaction can be sent. This response code appears under the CR column in the ECCX report;
if a debit reversal is required, Central 1芒聙聶s system sends a reversal request to the banking system; and
the banking system responds with a code which determines if the reverse debit request was successful. This code appears under the RDR column on the ECCX report. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Response Codes on the ECCX Report
When an Interac e-Transfer customer attempts to receive or cancel an Interac
e-Transfer via online banking,
Interac sends a response code to Central 1 which determines the status of the Interac
e-Transfer transaction. This response code appears in the DR column on the ECCX report;
if the Interac
e-Transfer transaction is approved for deposit, Central 1 sends a request to the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system to credit the customer芒聙聶s account; and
the banking system sends a response code to the online banking platform which indicates if the credit request is approved or denied. If the credit request is denied, the response code appears under the CR column in the ECCX report for the associated transaction.
Note: 	If the banking host denies the credit, the Interac
e-Transfer transaction has already been completed at Interac. The customer will see a successful message but won芒聙聶t receive the funds. The financial institution will have an outage and will need to post the transaction manually to the customer芒聙聶s account.
The following tables summarize which types of response codes will appear under the DR, CR, and RDR columns of the ECCX report:
Customer sending Interac e-Transfer
DR
Bank Host Response Code
CR
Interac Response Code
RDR
Bank Host Response Code
Customer receiving Interac e-Transfer
DR
Interac Response Code
CR
Bank Host Response Code
RDR
N/A
See the following documents for response codes and their descriptions:
Response Codes ISO 8583 Integration,
Form 9346
Response Codes ISO 20022 Integration,
Form 9347
END
